content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a placeholder ("TODO"), while location isn't even used anywhere in the code (App/Info.plist:6-9).
Pierre: Apple requires purpose strings to clearly describe *why* the specific feature needs the permission — a placeholder "TODO" string will almost certainly cause outright rejection, and the camera string is too generic given the scanner-specific use case in ScannerView.swift. Fix the camera string to something like "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents" and either remove the unused location key or write a real purpose string tied to an actual location feature.
